name: Pizza with Tuna, Basil, and Pine Nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the yeast with 3/4 cup lukewarm water. Let stand until doubled in volume. If it doesn't rise, discard and start with fresh yeast.
2. Add 1 1/2 cups high-gluten flour, salt, and extra virgin olive oil to the yeast mixture using a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Process until a dough forms a ball (about 20 seconds).
3. Turn the dough out onto a smooth surface. Knead for a couple of minutes, adding more flour if needed.
4. Form the dough into a ball and place it in a greased bowl. Cover loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (2-2 1/2 hours).
5. Punch down the dough and turn onto a lightly floured surface. Knead briefly.
6. Divide dough into two equal portions. Roll each portion out on a floured surface to fit a 12-inch pizza pan.
7. Brush the rolled-out dough with olive oil. Let rise for an additional hour.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
9. In a large frying pan, heat extra virgin olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and cook until fragrant.
10. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until softened, about 20 minutes.
11. Stir in tuna fish and cook for another minute.
12. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
13. Spread the tuna mixture evenly on half of the dough.
14. Sprinkle pine nuts and remaining olive oil over the tuna mixture.
15. Top with fresh basil leaves.
16. Bake until crust is golden brown (25-30 minutes).
